ddls:
CREATE TABLE "model_performance" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance record */,
  "iteration_time" REAL /* Time taken for the iteration */,
  "mean_kl" REAL /* Mean KL divergence */,
  "max_kl" REAL /* Max KL divergence */,
  "perplexity" REAL /* Perplexity */,
  "average_return" REAL /* Average return */,
  "loss_after" REAL /* Loss after training */,
  "min_return" REAL /* Minimum return */,
  "num_trajectories" INTEGER /* Number of trajectories */,
  "explained_variance" REAL /* Explained variance */,
  "loss_before" REAL /* Loss before training */,
  "time" REAL /* Time elapsed */,
  "std_return" REAL /* Standard deviation of return */,
  "entropy" REAL /* Entropy */,
  "max_return" REAL /* Maximum return */,
  "average_policy_std" REAL /* Average policy standard deviation */,
  "total_exec_time" REAL /* Total execution time */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "model_version" TEXT /* Version of the model */,
  "training_status" TEXT /* Status of the training (e.g., in_progress, completed, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "research_papers" (
  "paper_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each paper */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the paper */,
  "authors" TEXT /* Authors of the paper */,
  "publication_date" TEXT /* Date of publication */,
  "doi" TEXT /* Digital Object Identifier (DOI) */,
  "abstract" TEXT /* Abstract of the paper */,
  "paper_type" TEXT /* Type of paper (e.g., conference, journal) */,
  "publication" TEXT /* Name of the publication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("paper_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_paper_associations" (
  "association_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each association */,
  "model_performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model performance record */,
  "paper_id" INTEGER /* ID of the research paper */,
  "association_type" TEXT /* Type of association (e.g., cited, referenced) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("association_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_paper_associations_model_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_performance_id") REFERENCES model_performance ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_paper_associations_paper_id FOREIGN KEY ("paper_id") REFERENCES research_papers ("paper_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "model_performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model performance record being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the record */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the record was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Location of access (e.g., country, city) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_model_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_performance_id") REFERENCES model_performance ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "iterations" (
  "iteration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each iteration */,
  "model_performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model performance record */,
  "iteration_number" INTEGER /* Iteration number */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the iteration */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the iteration */,
  "iteration_status" TEXT /* Status of the iteration (e.g., completed, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("iteration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_iterations_model_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_performance_id") REFERENCES model_performance ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "scenarios" (
  "scenario_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each scenario */,
  "model_performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model performance record */,
  "scenario_name" TEXT /* Name of the scenario */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the scenario */,
  "scenario_type" TEXT /* Type of scenario (e.g., real-world, simulated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("scenario_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_scenarios_model_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_performance_id") REFERENCES model_performance ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration */,
  "model_performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model performance record */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration */,
  "hyperparameters" TEXT /* Hyperparameters used in the configuration */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of model (e.g., RL, supervised) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_configurations_model_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_performance_id") REFERENCES model_performance ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "training_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "model_performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model performance record */,
  "data_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the dataset (e.g., online, offline) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_training_data_model_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_performance_id") REFERENCES model_performance ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_evaluation" (
  "evaluation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each evaluation */,
  "model_performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model performance record */,
  "evaluation_metric" TEXT /* Name of the evaluation metric (e.g., accuracy, F1 score) */,
  "evaluation_score" REAL /* Score of the evaluation metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("evaluation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_evaluation_model_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_performance_id") REFERENCES model_performance ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_explainability" (
  "explainability_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each explainability technique */,
  "model_performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model performance record */,
  "technique" TEXT /* Name of the explainability technique (e.g., SHAP, LIME) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the explainability technique */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("explainability_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_explainability_model_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_performance_id") REFERENCES model_performance ("id")
)
